subject: refactor(debug): reorder debugLog.ts top-down, no forward references
body:
The file had helpers used on lines 199/230/239/273 but defined on
lines 297/320/309 — a scavenger hunt for any reader trying to follow
the control flow. Every call worked via function hoisting, but
reading debugLog top-down now matches execution order.

New structure (explicit section dividers in the source):
  1. Imports
  2. Public types (DebugSource, DebugSeverity, DebugDetails, DebugEntry)
  3. Pure helpers (safeStringify, errDetails, redactUrls)
  4. Pub/sub state + notifyAll
  5. Public API (debugAdd, debugGetEntries, debugGetCounts,
     debugClear, debugSubscribe)
  6. Environment + report (consumes helpers from section 3)
  7. Global error listener registration (consumes debugAdd +
     errDetails)
  8. installConsoleCapture() + boot log

No behaviour change — the order matters to readers, not to runtime.
tsc 0, jest 83/83, expo web build clean.

Tags: debug, refactor, readability
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: debug-pill-hardening
